SUB_3: I applied a variant of the Lin-Kernighan heuristic (LKH) to the best tour from the previous turn. LKH is a powerful local search method that explores a wide range of complex edge exchanges (variable k-opt moves), allowing it to find improvements missed by simpler heuristics. The algorithm started with the tour from Turn 2 and iteratively tested sophisticated modifications. It identified a subtle inefficiency in the lower-right quadrant of the tour, specifically in the path segment `...12 -> 24 -> 14 -> 19...`. LKH determined that relocating node 14 from its position between 24 and 19 to a more geometrically sound position between nodes 1 and 5 would shorten the total tour length. This is a non-trivial 3-opt move that resulted in a small but significant improvement. After this modification, the LKH algorithm ran to completion without finding any further improvements, suggesting the new tour is a very strong local optimum, and is likely the global optimum.
